entries.name: Cross
entries.detail: Season 2 premieres on Prime Video Feb. 11
entries.description: “Cross” Season 2 is bigger, darker, and more complex. The stakes feel real from the first episode. The characters you thought you have figured out come back with layers, secrets, and choices that will shock you. If you like a season that doesn't play it safe, this one delivers in a major way. — Myra

entries.name: Wuthering Heights
entries.description: Isn't it crazy that Emerald Fennell invented yearning? The Saltburn director's take on Emily Brontë's classic novel will shine a new light on Catherine and Heathcliff's love story — one that Fennell hopes will be “this generation's Titanic.” Margot Robbie said that some scenes with co-star Jacob Elordi made her “weak at the knees,” and if the trailers are any indication, we can assume fans will feel the same way. An epic romance sure to break our hearts perfectly timed to Valentine's Day ... what's not to love? — Hillary

entries.name: Good Luck, Have Fun, Don’t Die
entries.description: Can anyone save humanity from A.I. gone berserk? The real world answer is “no,” of course. But in Good Luck, Have Fun, Don’t Die, Sam Rockwell attempts to convince the patrons of an L.A. diner that he’s been sent from the future to save us. When director Gore Verbinski (whose genre-spanning filmography includes The Ring, Rango, and the first three Pirates of the Caribbean movies) spoke to IMDb last year, he shared three eclectic inspirations for his comedic sci-fi thriller. -- James

entries.name: 56 Days
entries.detail: Series premieres on Prime Video Feb. 18
entries.description: I know the “56 Days” novel was a lockdown phenom, its thrills coming from a short-lived, turbulent romance that yields a grizly discovery. Book readers, is it at all fair to say this adaptation plays like a Colleen Hoover story, but with a decomposing body at the center of the drama? Prime Video’s series wisely jettisoned the book’s pandemic setting, which is reason #2 I’m going to give it a chance. The main factor: Two women with supersolid TV backgrounds are the co-creators; if the show was created by dudes, it’d’ve been a pass for me. -- Arno

entries.name: How to Make a Killing
entries.description: Glen Powell doesn’t live in Hollywood, but his A-list slot remains secure with this rich-people satire from writer-director John Patton Ford. (Maybe you saw Ford's Emily the Criminal with Aubrey Plaza.) How to Make a Killing features Powell as a blue collar type who is cool with picking off his relatives in order to walk away with the family fortune. Margaret Qualley in accomplice mode could steal all scenes, for sure. A24 is behind the movie, and it feels like the Marty Supreme awards machine might’ve had an effect on the promotion of Powell’s bad-guy turn. — Arno

entries.name: The Last Thing He Told Me
entries.detail: Season 2 premieres on Apple TV Feb. 20
entries.description: Season 2 of “The Last Thing He Told Me” picks up five years after Season 1, with Hannah (Jennifer Garner) still trying to solve the mystery of her husband's disppearance — and reappearance — while trying to keep her stepdaugher out of harm's way. Is it even possible to stay safe when the mob is after you? That remains to be seen, but if there's anyone who could manage, it's Jennifer Garner in badass mama bear mode. Meanwhile, I'm still thinking about how Jen told us they call Nikolaj Coster-Waldau “daddy” on set... — Hillary

entries.name: Redux Redux
entries.description: Even if you think you’re sick of movies about the multiverse, give Kevin and Matthew McManus’ Redux Redux a shot. This sci-fi thriller goes hard from the first frame and never lets up till the exposive ending. Michaela McManus stars as a mother who travels across parallel universes avenging the murder of her daughter again and again and again … This was my favorite film at last year's SXSW Film Festival, and I'm thrilled to see this getting a deserved theatrical release. — James

entries.name: Paradise
entries.detail: Season 2 premieres on Hulu and Disney+ Feb. 23
entries.description: “It was never about the bunker...” Wait, what? Season 1 was literally all about the bunker.  We're used to this, though, as “Paradise” likes to bait and switch the audience in a good way, so I'm hoping by the end of the season the new tagline will be something like “It was never about the aliens...” Sterling K. Brown returns as Xavier Collins, Secret Service agent and determined dad who broke bunker containment last season to see the truth for himself. Although this series gives off “Lost” vibes, showrunner Dan Fogelman has promised a three-season story arc, which has been moving at quite a clip, so I have no doubt he's going to stick the landing. — Vanessa

entries.name: The Fall and Rise and Reggie Dinkins
entries.detail: Series premieres on NBC Feb. 23 (also streaming on Peacock)
entries.description: I freely admit I was more excited for this comedy series, which stars Tracy Morgan as a disgraced footballer who teams up with a documentarian (Daniel Radcliffe) to repair his rep, when I thought Tina Fey was its creator. (Turns out the show’s from two of her longtime creative partners; Fey’s in exec producer mode.) Early reviews say give the show a few episodes, it seems to find its comedic footing quickly. While the show will air on NBC, it’ll be available on Peacock for those of us who cut the TV cord long ago. — Arno

entries.name: Survivor
entries.detail: Season 50 premieres Feb. 25 on CBS (and streams on Paramount+)
entries.description: Does Boston Rob want viewers at home to be in charge of whether he gets rice after a challenge win? That’s one of a few new scenarios that’ll play out as one of the longest-running reality competitions celebrates 50 seasons by bringing back an all all-star cast and letting fans make key decisions throughout the game. Ozzy, Cirie, and that guy who now makes hit TV series are among the 24 contestants who will outwit one another over 26 days. It’s nuts to know the two Season 49 players returning for the anniversary had under two weeks’ rest between seasons. — Arno

entries.name: Bridgerton
entries.detail: Season 4, Part 2 premieres Feb. 26 on Netflix
entries.description: Swoony season continues as Netflix drops the final four episodes this month, wrapping up the Cinderella story with the inevitable happy ending. I love those romance novel requirements! Based on the novel “An Offer from a Gentleman,” this season delivers a lot of Bridgerton family drama, continuing the budding romance between Bridgerton's matriarch Lady Violet and Lady Danbury's brother, Lord Marcus Anderson, and exploring the fallout from Penelope's reveal as Lady Whistledown. Will we also get some hint as to which of the Bridgertons might find love next season? We will see! — Vanessa

entries.name: Scream 7
entries.description: Would Woodsboro be as fun without half of the Core Four? The Carpenter sisters, who were played by Jenna Ortega and Melissa Barerra in the last two Scream movies, were conjoined hearts of the truly revitalized franchise; without them, it’ll be curious to see what Scream 7 delivers. Will it be enough for fans to have Sidney Prescott back home again? The movie generated headlines due to the snafus that led to Barerra and Ortega’s departures, and the potentially related pre-production dramas that caused the Radio Silence director team to bail. It seems like Kevin Williamson, the man who started it all, is paying the best fan service he can in the director’s seat, going so far as announcing that Dewey (David Arquette) will return in some form. — Arno

entries.name: Monarch: Legacy of Monsters
entries.detail: Season 2 premieres on Apple TV Feb. 27
entries.description: Father/son duo Kurt and Wyatt Russell return for Season 2 of “Monarch: Legacy of Monsters,” where we wake up on Skull Island amidst interdimensional chaos. It turns out Godzilla and Kong were the least of our worries as a bigger, badder fox emerges from the proverbial hen house. Only this time, that hen house is the deep sea? Tune in for the monster battles, stay for the score that never fails keep me on the edge of my seat. — Hillary

entries.name: Dracula
entries.description: Dracula still wants your blood and soul, but he’s also totally looking for love in Luc Besson’s take on the one true vampire origin tale. Caleb Landry Jones will pine for Zoë Bleu (Rosanna Arquette’s daughter, fyi) in what looks to be one of the more visually stimulating releases of the year. Harder-core horror fans might be less attracted to Besson’s love story, but his latest might make for a good double feature with Guillermo del Toro’s Frankenstein. The movie is getting an indie release in the US, so there’s a chance for Dracula to find love and success on streaming. — Arno

entries.name: Whistle
entries.description: Dafne Keen (Logan, “His Dark Materials”) and Sophie Nélisse (“Yellowjackets”) join a group of teens bound together by two essential high school rites of passage: detention and an ancient Aztec death whistle that summons death for anyone foolish enough to blow it. Director Corin Hardy (The Nun) and writer Owen Egerton have conjured a throwback fright fest that draws inspiration from iconic ‘80s films, from The Lost Boys to The Breakfast Club. — James

entries.name: The ’Burbs
entries.description: Keke Palmer follows up One of Them Days (the funniest movie of 2025) with this cozy mystery series, a remake of the Joe Dante/Tom Hanks ‘80s cult classic that had a lot to say about the sinister side of suburban life. Keke can carry a comedy completely on her own, but this supporting cast, which includes sitcom icon Julia Duffy and the eternal Mark Proksch from “What We Do in the Shadows,” has my head fully turned. — Arno

plot_text.plain_text: Series adaptation of James Patterson novels about the complicated and brilliant detective, Alex Cross.
A passionate and tumultuous love story set against the backdrop of the Yorkshire moors, exploring the intense and destructive relationship between Heathcliff and Catherine Earnshaw.

plot_text.plain_text: A "Man From the Future" arrives at a diner in Los Angeles where he must recruit the precise combination of disgruntled patrons to join him on a one-night quest to save the world from the terminal threat of a rogue artificial intelligence

original_title_text.text: Good Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die

plot_text.plain_text: After a chance supermarket encounter, Oliver and Ciara embark on an intense relationship. When a savagely murdered corpse surfaces 56 days later, the investigation delves into their turbulent love story, revealing grim secrets.
Disowned at birth by his obscenely wealthy family, blue-collar Becket Redfellow will stop at nothing to reclaim his inheritance, no matter how many relatives stand in his way.

plot_text.plain_text: A woman forms an unexpected relationship with her 16-year-old stepdaughter while searching for the truth about why her husband has mysteriously disappeared.

original_title_text.text: The Last Thing He Told Me

plot_text.plain_text: Irene Kelly travels through parallel universes, repeatedly killing her daughter's murderer. As she becomes consumed by vengeance, her humanity hangs in the balance.

node.text: Untitled Dan Fogelman/Sterling K. Brown/Hulu Project

plot_text.plain_text: A Secret Service agent investigates the murder of a former president in a seemingly peaceful community.

node.text: Survivor: Cagayan - Brawn vs Brains vs Beauty

plot_text.plain_text: A reality show where a group of contestants are stranded in a remote location with little more than the clothes on their back. The lone survivor of this contest takes home a million dollars.
The eight close-knit siblings of the Bridgerton family look for love and happiness in London high society.
When a new Ghostface killer emerges in the town where Sidney Prescott has built a new life, her darkest fears are realized as her daughter becomes the next target.

node.text: Monarch: El legado de los monstruos
モナーク：レガシー・オブ・モンスターズ
君主計畫：神秘組織與怪獸之謎

plot_text.plain_text: Set after the battle between Godzilla and the Titans, revealing that monsters are real, follows one family's journey to uncover its buried secrets and a legacy linking them to Monarch.

node.text: Drácula: Uma História de Amor Eterno

plot_text.plain_text: When a 15th-century prince denounces God after the loss of his wife he inherits an eternal curse: he becomes Dracula. Condemned to wander the centuries, he defies fate and death, guided by a single hope - to be reunited with his lost love.

A misfit group of unwitting high school students stumble upon a cursed object, an ancient Aztec Death Whistle. They discover that blowing the whistle and the terrifying sound it emits will summon their future deaths to hunt them down.
A young couple moves back to the husband's childhood home, only to face threats from new neighbors and uncover dark secrets of their cul-de-sac.
